Patch Set 21: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(8 inline comments)

The patch looks pretty good, and I'm definitely happy to see this functionality being added, since this is something that users need to do on occasion, and running --writeconf is not ideal.

One important note is that this patch conflicts with the OSD API changes being made in the MGS in the LU-1302 patch series (http://review.whamcloud.com/3631) which has already started landing.  I don't think the conflict should be too bad to resolve, but there is definitely some overlap.

Patch Set 22: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(12 inline comments)

Hi Artem,

Please run the following commands from the top-level Lustre tree checkout:

ln -sf ../../build/commit-msg .git/hooks/
ln -sf ../../build/prepare-commit-msg .git/hooks/

Then, at each git commit time this will run the build/prepare-commit-msg script from the currently Lustre tree to check the code changes for style errors using build/checkpatch.pl, and add comments at the bottom of the commit message with any warnings or errors.

Thanks.
Uploaded patch set 23.

Please update all CERROR messages to be in form: CERROR("%s: message: rc = %d\n", obd_name, rc);
It is OK to use fsname instead of obd_name when error is not related to device. Note, I didn't point out all places to fix.
Another style issue is using spaces instead of tabs, please, fix that too. Other comments are inline.

Patch Set 28: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(5 inline comments)

Artem, it looks like your commit hook are not working as there are multiple style issues.
Check your .git/hooks/, there is known bug with symlinks, it should be so:
commit-msg -> ../../build/commit-msg
prepare-commit-msg -> ../../build/prepare-commit-msg

note ../.. here but not just single ../

If hooks are set up properly you will get style warnings in commit message

one more note

parents.subject: LU-1187 tests: Add dne specific tests to sanityN

commit.subject: LU-1430 mgs: "replace_nids" lctl command added
commit.message:
LU-1430 mgs: "replace_nids" lctl command added

Currently after device address is changed tunefs
should be started with "writeconf" options. This
erase configuration logs for the filesystem that
this server is part of, and regenerate them. This
is very dangerous. All clients must be unmounted
and servers  for  this filesystem should be stopped.
All targets (OSTs/MDTs) must then be restarted to
regenerate the logs.  No clients should be started
until all targets have restarted. The targets start
order is also important. Wrong order can be cause
of failure.

This patch adds "replace_nids" command to lctl
that should be executed when only MGS server
started. This command replace nids and uuid for
given device. Command can be used to change
OST or MDT nids.

Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-397
Reviewed-by: Alexey Lyashkov <alexey_lyashkov@xyratex.com>
Reviewed-by: Nathan Rutman <nathan.rutman@xyratex.com>
Signed-off-by: Artem Blagodarenko <artem_blagodarenko@xyratex.com>
Change-Id: I74b72281ce3b5e48188735035e58250596a16bf7
